steps: A hemisphere is half a sphere. Volume of a sphere = \(\frac{4}{3}\pi r^3\), so hemisphere volume = \(\frac{2}{3}\pi r^3\).

• Choice A — '14,400': Far too small; this is roughly \(\pi r^2\) for a small radius. ✗
  • Choice B — '57,700': Too small by a factor of about 20; perhaps a miscalculation using \(r^2\) instead of \(r^3\). ✗
  • Choice C — '898,200': Close but not the nearest; this would correspond to using \(\pi \approx 3\) only. ✗
  • Choice D — '1,197,500': Matches the computed value \(\approx 1{,}197{,}500\). ✓
That matches choice D — D is correct.
